perturbation.original_text: COFS shall provide STW rent free site leases for the commercially reasonable amounts of surface area required for CRA pumping and filling stations located on the Property.
perturbation.changed_text: COFS shall provide STW rent free site leases for the surface area deemed necessary by COFS for CRA pumping and filling stations located on the Property.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes 'commercially reasonable amounts' to 'surface area deemed necessary by COFS'. This creates ambiguity and contradiction because the original phrasing implied an objective standard of reasonableness, while the modified phrasing gives COFS sole discretion, which is a subjective standard. This impacts Section 13, where STW's maintenance and operation of wells are supposed to be conducted in a 'businesslike manner consistent with good and economical practices', as COFS could now arbitrarily limit the surface area, hindering STW's ability to comply with businesslike operations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STW will construct, maintain, operate, own, and repair (as needed) the Existing CRA Well, the Replacement CRA Well and any Additional Wells, and in doing so, and in the wells' operation and repair, STW shall: a. conduct all work and operations in a businesslike manner consistent with good and economical practices and with due regard for good land management, damage prevention and environmental protection; b. comply with all federal, state, and local laws, regulations and requirements governing the development, production, collection, transportation, and disposal of the water (and any waste or reject water); c. allow COFS or its authorized representatives to enter upon the premises at reasonable times to be arranged in advance of entry; and d. plug all wells, when abandoned, in accordance with the applicable regulatory requirements of the Middle Pecos Groundwater Conservation District, TDLR, and/or TCEQ.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a subjective standard ('surface area deemed necessary by COFS') where the original text implied an objective standard of 'commercially reasonable amounts'. This directly conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text' which requires STW to operate in a 'businesslike manner consistent with good and economical practices'. COFS's arbitrary limitation of surface area, as now permitted by the changed text, could prevent STW from operating in such a businesslike manner, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: STW shall pay COFS a seventeen percent (17%) royalty of the price STW receives per 1,000 gallons of CRA water produced from the Property and sold.
perturbation.changed_text: STW shall pay COFS a royalty based on a percentage to be determined annually of the price STW receives per 1,000 gallons of CRA water produced from the Property and sold. The initial royalty percentage is seventeen percent (17%).
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces uncertainty regarding the royalty percentage after the first year. While the initial royalty is 17%, the phrase 'percentage to be determined annually' allows for changes in subsequent years, creating a conflict with the original text which specified a fixed 17% royalty throughout the agreement's term. This contradicts the term of agreement and the payment obligations of STW
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the term. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a variable royalty percentage, 'percentage to be determined annually', which directly contradicts the existing agreement's term of thirty (30) years, specified in the 'Contradicted Text', by allowing for potential modifications to the royalty rate, creating uncertainty about the payment obligations of STW over the term of the agreement. This ambiguity could lead to disputes and unenforceability, given that the original text implied a fixed 17% royalty throughout the term. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Party claiming the Force Majeure shall use due diligence to resume performance at the earliest practicable time, and such suspension shall not extend for a period longer than twenty-four (24) months from the date notice of the Force Majeure is provided without payment as set forth under Section 15(a) of this AGREEMENT by STW, or the option to terminate this AGREEMENT by STW if COFS is the party asserting the Force Majeure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Party claiming the Force Majeure shall use reasonable efforts to resume performance when reasonably possible, and such suspension may extend indefinitely from the date notice of the Force Majeure is provided, subject to ongoing review by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause sets a clear limit of 24 months for Force Majeure without invoking specific financial consequences or termination rights. By contrast, the revised clause eliminates the time limit and replaces 'due diligence' with 'reasonable efforts', and 'earliest practicable time' with 'when reasonably possible', introducing ambiguity and subjectivity. This creates a direct conflict with Section 3, where a definite term of 30 years for the agreement is established, because a potentially indefinite suspension due to Force Majeure effectively overrides or negates the agreed-upon term length.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the term. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing for an indefinite suspension due to Force Majeure, conflicting directly with the original agreement's stipulated 30-year term as specified in 'Contradicted Text'. The original text, '...this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date...', is rendered uncertain by the new condition, meeting the conditions specified in the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
